title: Policy viability under scrutiny
desc: Analysts have expressed caution over the feasibility of Prime Minister Anutin Charnvirakul's "Five Pillars" policy roadmap, unveiled under the slogan "Promises Delivered," citing a lack of clearly differentiated initiatives, the absence of a "signature" policy, and the lack of an explicit commitment to constitutional amendments.

title: New rules to boost data centre power
desc: The Energy Regulatory Commission (ERC) has finished drafting requirements for investors in data centres and other electricity-intensive businesses, mandating bank guarantees or collateral to secure power purchases.

title: Tourism sector seeking clear policies
desc: Tourism operators have criticised the government for lacking clear policies to address the debt burden and improve the competitiveness of small and medium-sized operators.

title: U-Tapao plan eyes 3m travellers a year
desc: The launch of the U-Tapao Airport and Eastern Airport City (EECa) development plan aims to transform U-Tapao into a new aviation hub capable of handling three million passengers annually.

title: Drugs link to rising youth mental illness
desc: Methamphetamine, cannabis and kratom remain key drivers of drug abuse among youth, with mental health complaints linked to drug use rising to 77.44%, according to research released this week.

title: Eastern border crossings to stay shut for now: PM
desc: Border crossings in Trat and Chanthaburi will remain closed, with no diplomatic talks or timeline set for reopening, says Prime Minister Anutin Charnvirakul.

title: Huge turnout battles rash of wildfires
desc: Wildfires intensified across northern and western Thailand, with 2,165 hotspots detected, as officials and an army of 25,000 fire-fighters escalated aerial and ground operations to contain spreading blazes.

title: Fuel crisis upends state's fiscal plans
desc: The energy crisis may pose a challenge for the government in pursuing its fiscal plan, which aims to reduce the fiscal deficit to no more than 3% of GDP, increase revenue through a higher value-added tax (VAT), and keep public debt below 70% of GDP, according to the Finance Ministry.

title: New push to keep fares low
desc: The government has approved a 2.06 billion baht support package for the transport sector, aiming to cushion the impact of rising fuel prices, stabilise fares and ease pressure on household living costs.

title: Calls mount for energy pricing rejig
desc: A coalition of civic groups has called on Energy Minister Akanat Promphan to reform the energy pricing structure, enact laws to reclaim excess refinery profits, and liberalise rooftop solar installation for households.

title: Agency raises diesel price ceiling in cost formula
desc: The Comptroller General's Department has raised the diesel price ceiling used in calculating reference prices for public construction projects to no more than 69.99 baht per litre, in order to reflect the rising costs of government construction works resulting from the war in the Middle East.

title: Ministry mulls shelving fuel loan
desc: The Energy Ministry is weighing whether to abandon plans to borrow 150 billion baht to support the fast-depleting Oil Fuel Fund, following a breakthrough in negotiations with oil refineries to lower diesel prices.

title: K-Research foresees stagflation risk in H2
desc: Thailand is expected to enter a state of stagflation late in the second quarter and the beginning of the third quarter of this year as businesses begin material restocking while oil prices remain high amid ongoing conflicts in the Middle East, according to Kasikorn Research Centre (K-Research).

title: Fertiliser supply set to last until May
desc: The Commerce Minister says the current fertiliser supply is expected to last until May, and at present no producers have requested approval to increase prices.

title: Oman to deepen energy, defence ties
desc: Thailand and Oman have agreed to deepen cooperation in defence and energy following an official visit by Deputy Prime Minister and Foreign Affairs Minister Sihasak Phuangketkeow.

title: Norathip draws new fire on unrest
desc: Internal Security Operations Command (Isoc) Region 4 has accused insurgent groups in the deep South of distorting Islamic teachings to justify violence, as backlash intensifies against the Fourth Army Area commander over remarks about religious schools.

title: Songkran just the tonic amid Mideast ructions
desc: The Songkran festival has played a crucial role in supporting and reviving the economy during a period of energy-related uncertainty, with expectations rising for further stimulus measures to sustain momentum, an academic said.

title: Fresh digital plan to fortify Thailand
desc: The Ministry of Digital Economy and Society (DES) has overhauled its strategy, aiming to position itself as the nation's centralised digital connectivity hub to strengthen Thailand against global uncertainties, cyberthreats and natural disasters, while ensuring transparent welfare distribution.

title: Initiative aims to update farming data landscape
desc: The National Statistical Office (NSO), in collaboration with the Ministry of Agriculture and Cooperatives, has launched the One Data initiative to modernise and integrate Thailand's fragmented agricultural data landscape.

title: Gulf opts to maintain KBank shareholding at 10% cap
desc: Gulf Development Plc has no plans to increase its shareholding in Kasikornbank (KBank) beyond 10%, partly due to additional regulatory requirements.

title: Nike takes a walk on the spicy side
desc: Arguably Thailand’s most celebrated season, Songkran just swept through — ushering in the Thai New Year with its signature water-soaked celebration. Tapping into that spirit, Nike drops a festive take on the Nike SB Dunk Low with the “Songkran” iteration.

title: New taste tinglers for your palate
desc: Bangkok is a city that never sleeps, at least in the food and beverage department. Blink and you’ll miss another new restaurant opening, a drink launch, or a new nightclub. Guru By Bangkok Post is making sure that even if you blink, you don’t miss out on the new taste tinglers that are worth mentioning, in Bangkok and beyond.

title: Beyond the ordinary
desc: Hong Kong is always a good escape from Bangkok. Though it may be another concrete jungle escape, there are plenty of sea views and greenery to feel like an escape.

title: Thai Gen Z travellers prefer short, frequent trips
desc: Thai Gen Z travellers are reshaping travel habits in 2026 by favouring short but frequent trips over traditional long holidays, with mental well-being and value for money emerging as top priorities, according to the online booking platform Agoda.

title: New movies out this week: April 16-22
desc: Seven new releases that hit cinemas in Thailand this week.

title: 囤油等涨价？泰国警方突袭多府油库，多家公司被查
desc: 泰国头条新闻社讯 4月16日，泰国司法部联合多部门召开发布会，披露警方调查发现：部分油库涉嫌人为控油、延迟出货，只为等涨价。全国已查获多起非法囤油、走私及违规售油案件，6家公司被要求作出解释。

title: 近500万人涌入！2026曼谷宋干节人数翻倍暴增
desc: 泰国头条新闻社讯 4月16日，曼谷市政府发言人阿瓦伦透露，官方已公布2026年曼谷宋干节（4月10日至15日）整体活动统计数据，并与2025年同期进行对比。

title: What does it cost to raise a family in Thailand?
desc: What does it cost to raise a family in Thailand? Legacy Thailand regularly ranks among the top destinations for expat families, but cost estimates online tend to assume you are moving as a single person or a couple.

title: Songkran finished but Pattaya is still going — Here is the full Wan Lai 2026 schedule
desc: Songkran finished but Pattaya is still going — Here is the full Wan Lai 2026 schedule Legacy Songkran in Pattaya runs longer than anywhere else in Thailand.

title: Chinese woman stages her own kidnapping to extort money from father
desc: Thai police arrested four Chinese nationals in Samut Prakan after a Chinese woman staged her own kidnapping with her boyfriend and two other men to extort money from her father.

title: 外国游客吐槽“泰国是个骗局”，反转结局让泰国网友笑疯
desc: 泰国头条新闻社讯 近日，一段标题为“Thailand is a scam（泰国是个骗局）”的视频在社交平台上意外爆红，迅速引发全球网友关注。

title: 泰国商业部拟调整架构应对能源与成本上行压力
desc: 泰国头条新闻社讯 据泰媒报道，4月16日，泰国副总理兼商业部部长素帕琪表示，将加快对商业部工作架构进行大幅调整，以应对正在冲击泰国经济的双重危机。

title: Japan pledges US$10 billion to tackle energy crisis in Asia
desc: Japan pledged US$10 billion to support Asian countries in addressing the energy crisis, citing the close links and interdependence created by supply chains.

title: 宋干节期间156起交通事故 泰方严控返程客运零酒精
desc: 4月16日，泰国防灾减灾厅（DDPM）道路安全指挥中心通报宋干节期间道路事故统计。15日当天事故156起，23人死亡。

title: 4 Pattaya taxi riders and bar guards arrested for attacking 2 foreigners
desc: Four Thai men, including motorcycle taxi riders and bar security guards, were arrested after a viral video showed them attacking two foreigners during Songkran celebrations in Pattaya.

title: 自导自演绑架案！4名中国公民在泰被捕
desc: 泰国头条新闻社讯 一起引发中泰警方高度关注的“跨国绑架案”近日真相大白。一名19岁中国女子的父亲在支付逾11万元人民币赎金后，女儿仍未获释，遂向中国警方报案。泰国中央调查局接获中方协查请求后展开跨境调查，最终在北榄府一处出租屋内找到该女子，并逮捕4名中国籍涉案人员。令人震惊的是，这起所谓的绑架勒索案，竟是受害人自导自演、伙同他人共同欺骗其父亲的骗局。

title: 2 Frenchmen arrested for assault on Thai motorcyclist in Phuket Songkran celebrations
desc: 2 Frenchmen arrested for assault on Thai motorcyclist in Phuket Songkran celebrations Legacy A Thai man suffered serious injuries after he was attacked by Frenchmen in Phuket on April 13 after refusing to join a Songkran water fight.

title: Thailand video news | Songkran road toll climbs as return travel raises fresh safety concerns, Patong police detain tourists accused of targeting officers with water guns
desc: Thailand video news | Songkran road toll climbs as return travel raises fresh safety concerns, Patong police detain tourists accused of targeting officers with water guns The Thaiger In Thailand video news, Alex and Jay cover stories from across Thailand and Southeast Asia.

title: Phichit man reports sexual assault by women and transwomen during Songkran
desc: Phichit man reports sexual assault by women and transwomen during Songkran Legacy A man filed a police complaint after a group of women and transwomen sexually assaulted him during Songkran celebrations in Phichit province, leaving a hickey on his neck .

title: 5 things to do in Bangkok this weekend (April 17 to 19)
desc: 5 things to do in Bangkok this weekend (April 17 to 19) Legacy After the water fights , traffic, and noise of Songkran, Bangkok settles into a different rhythm this weekend.

title: 泰国2025年职场幸福感区域排名第三 倦怠与压力成关键挑战
desc: 泰国头条新闻社讯 4月16日，据泰媒报道，Jobsdb by SEEK作为泰国领先的在线求职平台，公布了2025年《职场幸福感报告》调查结果。

title: 2026年宋干节盛况空前 多家国际媒体聚焦世界级泼水节
desc: 泰国头条新闻社讯 4月15日，据泰媒报道，泰国总理府办公室发言人拉差达表示，2026年宋干节整体活动呈现热烈、盛大氛围，展现了泰国宋干节作为联合国教科文组织登记的文化遗产所具备的国际影响力。她称，宋干节获得多国认可，并吸引全球民众参与，涵盖传统礼俗、文化活动与娱乐项目等多个层面。 她指出，今年共有42家驻泰外国大使馆参与合作，包括英国、瑞士、丹麦、德国、印度、比利时、中国、菲律宾、越南、澳大利亚和美国等，各大使馆共同制作宣传内容，以更现代、更易传播的方式呈现泰国文化特色，进一步触达全球受众。 同时，多家国际媒体机构，包括美联社、路透社、欧罗新闻和新华社，报道了多个重点区域的泼水活动氛围，尤其是考山路等地，游客十分密集。相关报道称，泰国宋干节是全球最盛大、最具色彩的节庆之一，体现了传统文化与当代生活方式的融合。北美媒体将宋干节描述为“世界级泼水节”，认为其对泰国经济与旅游业具有重要作用。 在安全管理方面，泰方称也获得外媒关注与肯定。例如《海峡时报》发布了“10条安全玩水规则”，包括禁止性骚扰、禁止未经同意抹粉、禁止使用高压水枪等，反映活动组织强调公众安全与秩序。 关于旅游 market 总体表现，泰国国家旅游局评估称，宋干节期间（2026年4月11日至15日）预计将带来全国旅游总收入超过303.5亿泰铢，其中入境外国游客约500000人，预计创造收入约81亿泰铢。政府发言人并表示，政府将持续推动
